anxiety: How often do you feel anxious during the day?
Do you experience panic attacks or sudden fear?
Are there specific situations that trigger your anxiety?
Do you have trouble sleeping because of your anxiety?
Does anxiety interfere with school, work, or daily tasks?
Have you noticed physical symptoms like a racing heart or sweating?
Do you avoid social situations due to anxiety?
Have you tried therapy, meditation, or stress techniques before?
Is your anxiety linked to any major life events or trauma?
Have you ever had thoughts of self-harm with your anxiety?
cancer: When were you first diagnosed with cancer?
Do you know what type of cancer you have?
Have you received treatments like chemotherapy, radiation, or surgery?
Do you currently have pain related to the cancer?
Has your appetite or weight changed significantly?
Have you noticed new lumps, swelling, or bleeding?
Are you experiencing fatigue or low energy?
Do you have regular follow-up appointments with an oncologist?
Does cancer treatment affect your daily life?
Have you had genetic testing or family history of cancer?
cardio: Do you experience shortness of breath during activity?
Have you noticed swelling in your legs or feet?
Do you have chest discomfort or tightness when exerting yourself?
Has a doctor diagnosed you with high blood pressure or heart disease?
Do you have a history of fainting or dizziness?
Are you currently on heart medications (like beta blockers or statins)?
Do you exercise regularly or have any activity limitations?
Do you have a family history of heart problems?
Have you ever had an ECG, echocardiogram, or stress test?
Do you feel palpitations or irregular heartbeats?
chest_pain: When did your chest pain start?
Does the pain spread to your arm, neck, or jaw?
Do you feel chest pain during activity or at rest?
Is the chest pain sharp, dull, or pressure-like?
Do you also experience sweating, nausea, or dizziness?
Have you ever had chest pain like this before?
Does resting, deep breathing, or position changes relieve the pain?
Do you have a history of heart or lung disease?
Do you smoke or have other risk factors for heart disease?
Have you recently experienced stress or anxiety with chest pain?
depression: How long have you been feeling down or hopeless?
Do you find it difficult to enjoy things you used to like?
Do you struggle to sleep or sleep too much?
Do you feel tired even after resting?
Do you have difficulty concentrating or making decisions?
Have you noticed changes in your appetite or weight?
Do you avoid social contact because of your mood?
Have you experienced feelings of guilt or worthlessness?
Have you had thoughts of hurting yourself?
Have you tried counseling, therapy, or medications for depression?
derm: What type of skin changes have you noticed (redness, bumps, scaling)?
Where on your body are the skin changes located?
Is the area itchy, painful, or burning?
Did the rash or lesion appear suddenly or gradually?
Have you tried any creams or treatments for the skin issue?
Do you have allergies or sensitive skin?
Have you started new medications, soaps, or foods?
Has the rash spread to other parts of the body?
Do you have a family history of eczema or psoriasis?
Have you been exposed to new environments (plants, pets, chemicals)?
digestive: Do you have nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea?
Have you experienced abdominal pain or bloating?
Do you notice changes in your bowel movements?
Do you have difficulty swallowing food or liquids?
Have you noticed blood in your stool or vomit?
Do you experience heartburn or acid reflux?
Has your appetite or weight changed?
Does the pain get worse after meals?
Do you have a history of ulcers or stomach infections?
Have you recently traveled or eaten unusual foods?
fever: What was your highest recorded temperature?
Do you also have chills, sweating, or night sweats?
Are you experiencing cough, sore throat, or body aches?
Have you been around anyone who was sick recently?
Do you have pain when urinating or frequent urination?
Have you recently traveled to areas with infections like malaria or dengue?
Does the fever come and go or stay constant?
Do you feel confused, weak, or faint with the fever?
Have you taken any medication to reduce the fever?
gi: Do you have frequent stomach pain or cramps?
Do you experience diarrhea or constipation?
Have you noticed blood or mucus in your stool?
Do you feel bloated or full after small meals?
Do certain foods trigger your stomach issues?
Do you have unexplained weight loss or poor appetite?
Do you feel acid reflux or burning in your chest?
Has your bowel habit suddenly changed?
Do you have a history of inflammatory bowel disease or IBS?
Do you have nausea or vomiting with your stomach issues?
infectious: What symptoms make you think this is an infection?
Have you had recent contact with someone who was sick?
Do you have cough, fever, or difficulty breathing?
Do you have diarrhea, nausea, or vomiting?
Do you notice swelling, redness, or pus in any area?
Are you experiencing night sweats or weight loss?
Do you have pain when urinating or unusual discharge?
Have you been tested for infections before?
Are you vaccinated for common infections like flu or COVID?
mental: What type of mental health challenge are you facing (stress, mood, etc.)?
Do you find it hard to manage daily tasks?
Do you often feel overwhelmed or restless?
Do you have problems focusing or remembering things?
Do you have support from friends or family?
Do you ever feel unsafe with your thoughts?
Have you sought therapy, counseling, or psychiatric care before?
Do you have trouble sleeping or frequent nightmares?
Do stressful events worsen your symptoms?
Have you been prescribed medication for your mental health?
migraine: How often do you get migraine headaches?
Do you see flashing lights or aura before a migraine?
Is the pain usually on one side of your head?
Do you feel nauseated or vomit during migraines?
Does light or noise make the migraine worse?
How long does each migraine attack last?
Do you take medications for migraines?
Do you notice triggers like stress, food, or lack of sleep?
Do migraines affect your ability to work or study?
Have migraines ever caused you to visit the emergency room?
msk: Do you have pain in muscles, joints, or bones?
Is the pain sharp, dull, or throbbing?
Do you feel stiffness after rest or in the morning?
Have you noticed swelling, redness, or warmth in joints?
Do you have difficulty walking or moving due to pain?
Does activity make the pain better or worse?
Do you have a history of arthritis or injury?
Do you hear popping or grinding in joints?
Do you feel weakness or numbness with the pain?
Have you tried physical therapy, exercise, or stretching?
neuro: Do you have weakness, numbness, or tingling in any body part?
Have you experienced seizures or blackouts?
Do you have difficulty speaking or understanding words?
Have you noticed vision changes or double vision?
Do you have problems with memory or concentration?
Have you experienced dizziness, loss of balance, or falls?
Do you have tremors or uncontrolled movements?
Do your symptoms appear suddenly or gradually?
Do you have a history of stroke or neurological disease?
Do you have headaches along with neurological symptoms?
onc: What type of cancer are you being evaluated for?
Do you currently have symptoms like lumps, pain, or bleeding?
Are you undergoing treatment like chemo or radiation?
Do you experience fatigue or lack of energy?
Do you have unexplained weight loss or poor appetite?
Are you having difficulty breathing, swallowing, or moving?
Do you have pain related to the cancer?
Has your doctor discussed the cancer stage with you?
Do you have family history of this type of cancer?
Have you had recent scans or blood tests for cancer?
osteoarthritis: Which joints are painful from osteoarthritis?
Do you feel stiffness in the morning or after inactivity?
Do you hear grinding or cracking in the joints?
Does exercise improve or worsen the pain?
Have you noticed swelling or warmth around the joint?
Do you use any assistive devices like canes or braces?
Has osteoarthritis limited your daily activities?
Do you take medications like NSAIDs for pain?
Do you do physical therapy or exercises for the joints?
Do you have a family history of arthritis?
peds: How old is the child experiencing symptoms?
Has the child had all routine vaccinations?
Is the child eating and drinking normally?
Does the child have fever, cough, or rash?
Has the child been unusually fussy, sleepy, or inactive?
Are there feeding or growth concerns?
Has the child been exposed to sick contacts at school/daycare?
Has the child had recent travel?
Has the child had past hospitalizations or chronic illnesses?
Do the symptoms improve with rest or home care?
rash: When did the rash first appear?
Where is the rash located on your body?
Is the rash itchy, painful, or burning?
Did the rash spread quickly or gradually?
Have you tried creams or lotions on the rash?
Have you started new medications, foods, or soaps?
Does sunlight, heat, or cold worsen the rash?
Have you had similar rashes before?
Do you have fever or joint pain with the rash?
Have others around you developed a similar rash?
resp: Do you have shortness of breath?
Do you have a persistent cough?
Do you cough up mucus or blood?
Do you wheeze or hear whistling when breathing?
Do you get breathless during exercise or rest?
Have you had recent chest infections or pneumonia?
Do you smoke or have history of lung disease?
Do you feel chest tightness with breathing?
Do you have fever, chills, or night sweats with breathing issues?
Have you used inhalers or breathing medications?
skin: What changes in your skin concern you (spots, dryness, moles)?
Have you noticed any new or changing moles?
Is your skin itchy, painful, or burning?
Have you had recent sunburns or tanning bed exposure?
Has your skin issue lasted more than a few weeks?
Do you have family history of skin conditions or cancer?
Have you started new medications or skin products?
Does the problem get worse in heat, cold, or stress?
Have you seen a dermatologist before?
travel: Did you receive travel vaccinations before your trip?
Were you exposed to mosquitoes, animals, or unsafe water?
Did you have fever, rash, or stomach upset after travel?
Did you take malaria prevention medication?
Did you eat street food or raw food abroad?
Have you been in areas with dengue, malaria, or typhoid risk?
Did anyone you traveled with also get sick?
Did you have diarrhea during or after travel?
Did you seek medical care abroad?
ulcer: Do you have burning or gnawing pain in your stomach?
Does the pain get worse when hungry or at night?
Do spicy or acidic foods worsen the pain?
Do you take painkillers like ibuprofen regularly?
Have you noticed black or bloody stools?
Do you have nausea or vomiting with the pain?
Have you been tested for H. pylori infection?
Have you had ulcers in the past?
Does milk or food relieve the ulcer pain?
Have you lost weight because of stomach pain?
